channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: [AINews] Is Harness Engineering real?
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on a central debate in AI engineering

item.title: User may experience errors in ChatGPT
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Conversations (Degraded performance)

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 5, 01:30 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 5, 01:30 UTCUpdate - Copilot coding agent mission control is fully restored. Tasks are...

item.title: Elevated Durable Objects and R2 API Latency
item.description: Mar 5, 01:30 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 5, 00:34 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating elevated latency affecting Durable Objects and R2 API requests in Western North America region. Some requests may experience timeouts or increase response times.

item.title: BYD to Debut Ocean Flagship Sedan Seal 08 Globally; Five-Minute Charge Adds 400 km
item.description: BYD unveiled the Seal 08 flagship sedan on March 5, 2026, featuring megawatt fast charging that adds 400 km of range in five minutes, rear-wheel steering, second-generation Blade Battery technology, and roof-mounted LiDAR for advanced driver assistance.

item.title: Qwen Hardware Head: "One-Sentence Task Completion" to Drive AI Glasses Demand
item.description: Qwen hardware head Song Gang revealed that AI glasses will reach an inflection point in 2026, with "one-sentence task completion" features like food ordering and ride-hailing rolling out by March, after the Qwen app hit 73 million daily active users during Chinese New Year.

item.title: Ant Group and Tsinghua Release AReaL v1.0 for One-Click Agent Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Ant Group and Tsinghua University open-sourced AReaL v1.0 on March 4, 2026, a reinforcement learning framework that enables AI agents to connect to training systems with just one API change, achieving up to 2.77x faster training speeds.

item.title: LightSail Tech Raises Nearly USD 41.2 Million in Four Rounds Within One Yea
item.description: LightSail Tech, founded by a 14-year Xiaomi veteran, secured nearly RMB 300 million (approx. USD 41.2 million) across four seed rounds in one year, attracting investments from Lenovo, Xpeng-affiliated Rockets Capital, and CATL-backed funds to develop AI wearables and Agent OS.

item.title: Some OpenAI models degraded in Copilot
item.description: Mar 5, 01:13 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 5, 01:13 UTCUpdate - The issues with our upstream model provider have been resolved, and...

item.title: Alibaba's Qwen Model Head Lin Junyang Resigns; Executives Hold Emergency All-Hands
item.description: Lin Junyang, head of Alibaba's Qwen large model team, abruptly resigned on March 4, 2026, prompting an emergency all-hands meeting with top executives and sparking concerns over talent retention in Alibaba's flagship AI division.

item.title: API Error Rates
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Fine-tuning (Operational) Files (Operational) Moderations (Operational) Codex (Operational) Audio (Operational) Realtime (Operational) Embeddings (Operational) Responses (Operational) Images (Operational) Batch...

item.title: Every Agent Needs a Box — Aaron Levie, Box
item.description: The reception to our recent post on Code Reviews has been strong. Catch up!

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard displaying incorrect API token type
item.description: Mar 4, 18:45 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 4, 18:17 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 4, 18:13 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue with the Cloudflare Dashboard where Account API Tokens are not being displayed...

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishing Platform Dismantled in Global Takedown
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was used to send fraudulent emails to over 500,000 organizations every month. The post Tycoon 2FA Phishing Platform Dismantled in Global Takedown appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: New LexisNexis Data Breach Confirmed After Hackers Leak Files
item.description: The hackers claim to have stolen 2GB of files, including 400,000 personal information records. The post New LexisNexis Data Breach Confirmed After Hackers Leak Files appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The hacktivist threat in the Middle East is highly lopsided, with two groups, Keymous+ and DieNet, driving...

item.title: CNI Degraded Performance
item.description: Mar 4, 16:30 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is currently investigating issues related to CNI. Impacted customers may see connectivity issues through Newark, NJ (EWR) data center. Customers using Equinix Cloud Connect in EWR may experience intermittent connectivity or loss of service. We are working...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Mar 4, 16:13 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 4, 15:58 UTCInvestigating - The earlier issues with Haiku 4.5 have reappeared.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 4, 15:57 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 4, 14:14 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Zurich Acquires Beazley in $11 Billion Deal to Lead Cyberinsurance
item.description: The deal awaits final shareholder and regulatory approvals and is expected to be completed in the second half of 2026. The post Zurich Acquires Beazley in $11 Billion Deal to Lead Cyberinsurance appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Hacker Conversations: Inti De Ceukelaire, Raging Against the Machine Creatively
item.description: A Belgian national, De Ceukelaire’ did not set out to be a hacker. Like many hackers he was born with the potential to become one and only gradually realized he is one. The post Hacker Conversations: Inti De Ceukelaire, Raging Against the Machine Creatively appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Mar 4, 14:55 UTCResolved - The incident for Haiku 4.5 has been between 6:45 PT / 14:45 UTC and 6:50 PT / 14:50 UTC.Mar 4, 14:47 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Bridging the operational AI gap
item.description: The transformational potential of AI is already well established. Enterprise use cases are building momentum and organizations are transitioning from pilot projects to AI in production. Companies are no longer just talking about AI; they are redirecting budgets and resources to make it happen. Many...

item.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Uses 23 Exploits Across Five Chains Targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
item.description: Google said it identified a "new and powerful" exploit kit dubbed Coruna (aka CryptoWaters) targeting Apple iPhone models running iOS versions between 13.0 and 17.2.1. The exploit kit featured five full iOS exploit chains and a total of 23 exploits, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) said....

item.title: The Download: Earth’s rumblings, and AI for strikes on Iran
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Listen to Earth’s rumbling, secret soundtrack The boom of a calving glacier. The crackling rumble of a wildfire. The roar of a surging storm front....

item.title: How Pirated Software Turns Helpful Employees Into Malware Delivery Agents
item.description: Employees seeking free versions of paid software may unknowingly install malware-laced “cracked” apps that can steal credentials, deploy cryptominers, or open the door to ransomware. The post How Pirated Software Turns Helpful Employees Into Malware Delivery Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Domain Registration unavailable
item.description: Mar 4, 12:14 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 4, 10:56 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 4, 09:38 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue which potentially impacts multiple customers for domain...

item.title: Cloudflare GraphQL API outage for DNS metrics
item.description: Mar 4, 12:00 UTCResolved - Cloudflare GraphQL API experienced an outage between 11:56 and 17:00 UTC affecting DNS metrics queries across all regions. This issue has been resolved.

item.title: New RFP Template for AI Usage Control and AI Governance
item.description: As AI becomes the central engine for enterprise productivity, security leaders are finally getting the green light — and the budget — to secure it. But there’s a quiet crisis unfolding in the boardroom: many organizations know they need "AI Governance," but they have no idea what they are actually...

item.title: Fake Laravel Packages on Packagist Deploy RAT on Windows, macOS, and Linux
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged malicious Packagist PHP packages masquerading as Laravel utilities that act as a conduit for a cross-platform remote access trojan (RAT) that's functional on Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. The names of the packages are listed below - nhattuanbl/lara-helper...

item.title: APT41-Linked Silver Dragon Targets Governments Using Cobalt Strike and Google Drive C2
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an advanced persistent threat (APT) group dubbed Silver Dragon that has been linked to cyber attacks targeting entities in Europe and Southeast Asia since at least mid-2024. "Silver Dragon gains its initial access by exploiting public-facing...

item.title: Federated Inference: Toward Privacy-Preserving Collaborative and Incentivized Model Serving
item.description: arXiv:2603.02214v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Inference (FI) studies how independently trained and privately owned models can collaborate at inference time without sharing data or model parameters. While recent work has explored secure and distributed inference from disparate...

item.title: Engineering Reasoning and Instruction (ERI) Benchmark: A Large Taxonomy-driven Dataset for Foundation Models and Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.02239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Engineering Reasoning and Instruction (ERI) benchmark is a taxonomy-driven instruction dataset designed to train and evaluate engineering-capable large language models (LLMs) and agents. This dataset spans nine engineering fields (namely: civil,...

item.title: SuperLocalMemory: Privacy-Preserving Multi-Agent Memory with Bayesian Trust Defense Against Memory Poisoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.02240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present SuperLocalMemory, a local-first memory system for multi-agent AI that defends against OWASP ASI06 memory poisoning through architectural isolation and Bayesian trust scoring, while personalizing retrieval through adaptive learning-to-rank...

item.title: Estimating Visual Attribute Effects in Advertising from Observational Data: A Deepfake-Informed Double Machine Learning Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.02359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Digital advertising increasingly relies on visual content, yet marketers lack rigorous methods for understanding how specific visual attributes causally affect consumer engagement. This paper addresses a fundamental methodological challenge:...

item.title: Can machines be uncertain?
item.description: arXiv:2603.02365v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The paper investigates whether and how AI systems can realize states of uncertainty. By adopting a functionalist and behavioral perspective, it examines how symbolic, connectionist and hybrid architectures make room for uncertainty. The paper...

item.title: RxnNano:Training Compact LLMs for Chemical Reaction and Retrosynthesis Prediction via Hierarchical Curriculum Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.02215v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chemical reaction prediction is pivotal for accelerating drug discovery and synthesis planning. Despite advances in data-driven models, current approaches are hindered by an overemphasis on parameter and dataset scaling. Some methods coupled with...

item.title: ATPO: Adaptive Tree Policy Optimization for Multi-Turn Medical Dialogue
item.description: arXiv:2603.02216v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effective information seeking in multi-turn medical dialogues is critical for accurate diagnosis, especially when dealing with incomplete information. Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) for these interactive scenarios is challenging due to the...

item.title: Is Retraining-Free Enough? The Necessity of Router Calibration for Efficient MoE Compression
item.description: arXiv:2603.02217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models scale capacity efficiently, but their massive parameter footprint creates a deployment-time memory bottleneck. We organize retraining-free MoE compression into three paradigms - Expert Pruning, Expert Editing, and...

item.title: Self-Play Only Evolves When Self-Synthetic Pipeline Ensures Learnable Information Gain
item.description: arXiv:2603.02218v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) make it plausible to build systems that improve through self-evolving loops, but many existing proposals are better understood as self-play and often plateau quickly. A central failure mode is that the loop synthesises...

item.title: NExT-Guard: Training-Free Streaming Safeguard without Token-Level Labels
item.description: arXiv:2603.02219v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed in streaming scenarios, rendering conventional post-hoc safeguards ineffective as they fail to interdict unsafe content in real-time. While streaming safeguards based on token-level supervised training...

item.title: A Zipf-preserving, long-range correlated surrogate for written language and other symbolic sequences
item.description: arXiv:2603.02213v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Symbolic sequences such as written language and genomic DNA display characteristic frequency distributions and long-range correlations extending over many symbols. In language, this takes the form of Zipf's law for word frequencies together with...

item.title: Universal Conceptual Structure in Neural Translation: Probing NLLB-200's Multilingual Geometry
item.description: arXiv:2603.02258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Do neural machine translation models learn language-universal conceptual representations, or do they merely cluster languages by surface similarity? We investigate this question by probing the representation geometry of Meta's NLLB-200, a 200-language...

item.title: Characterizing Memorization in Diffusion Language Models: Generalized Extraction and Sampling Effects
item.description: arXiv:2603.02333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autoregressive language models (ARMs) have been shown to memorize and occasionally reproduce training data verbatim, raising concerns about privacy and copyright liability. Diffusion language models (DLMs) have recently emerged as a competitive...

item.title: Detecting AI-Generated Essays in Writing Assessment: Responsible Use and Generalizability Across LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.02353v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Writing is a foundational literacy skill that underpins effective communication, fosters critical thinking, facilitates learning across disciplines, and enables individuals to organize and articulate complex ideas. Consequently, writing assessment...

item.title: RO-N3WS: Enhancing Generalization in Low-Resource ASR with Diverse Romanian Speech Benchmarks
item.description: arXiv:2603.02368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce RO-N3WS, a benchmark Romanian speech dataset designed to improve generalization in automatic speech recognition (ASR), particularly in low-resource and out-of-distribution (OOD) conditions. RO-N3WS comprises over 126 hours of transcribed...

item.title: [AINews] Anthropic @ $19B ARR, Qwen team leaves, Gemini and GPT bump up fast models
item.description: a quiet day

item.title: Claude Opus 4.6 Fast not appearing for some Copilot users
item.description: Mar 3, 20:31 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Copilot

item.title: Incident with Copilot and Actions
item.description: Mar 3, 20:09 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 3, 20:06 UTCUpdate - We're seeing recovery across all services. We're continuing to...

item.title: The elbow statistic: Multiscale clustering statistical significance
item.description: Selecting the number of clusters remains a fundamental challenge in unsupervised learning. Existing criteria typically target a single ``optimal'' partition, often overlooking statistically meaningful structure present at multiple resolutions. We introduce ElbowSig, a framework that formalizes the...
